LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARA TAHMASSIAN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. PERSONAL JURISDICTION MAY BE EXERCISED OVER DEFENDANT BASED UPON HIS MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA. WHEN THE CLAIM AROSE, DEFENDANT WAS DOMICILED IN CALIFORNIA AND EMPLOYED AS THE ASSOCIATE VICE CHANCELLOR FOR RESEARCH AT UCSF. THE LAWSUIT IS CLOSELY RELATED TO DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITIES OR CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT DOES NOT SHOW WHY IT WOULD BE UNREASONABLE FOR COURT TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Deny Motion By Ara Tahmassian To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-13-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PIRUZ VARGHA Motion to Deny Motion by Ara Tahmassian to Quash Service of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Appearances: Piruz Vargha, Plaintiff in Pro Per and moving party appearing; Dennis C. Huie, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich Voluck & Gonzo LLP (415) 402-0059 appearing for Defendant. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, not a proper motion. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LEW, ROBERT EATON, KEITH CUDABACK, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Appearances: Piruz Vargha, Plaintiff in Pro Per and opposing party appearing; Dennis C. Huie, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich Voluck & Gonzo LLP (415) 402-0059 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Personal jurisdiction may be exercised over defendant based upon his minimum contacts with California. When the claim arose, defendant was domiciled in California and employed as the associate vice chancellor for Research at UCSF. The lawsuit is closely related to defendant's activities or contacts with California. Defendant does not show why it would be unreasonable for court to exercise jurisdiction. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LEW, ROBERT EATON, KEITH CUDABACK, and, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Plaintiff Piruz Varga appearing in Pro Per and opposing party. Plaintiff in Pro Per failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Plaintiff to allege statutory basis for liability against the Regents & exhaustion of administrative remedies. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WILLIAM LEW, ROBERT EATON, KEITH CUDABACK, and, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Plaintiff Piruz, Varga, in Pro Per appearing and opposing party. Plaintiff in Pro Per failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted without leave to amend as to The Regents. Plaintiff cannot obtain punitives against The Regents, as a matter of law. See Gov't Code section 818. Granted with leave to amend as to individual defendants. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
